title: SpaceX enters the Nasdaq 100 and receives multiple bullish recommendations from Wall Street
summary: Elon Musk's company joins the tech index and analysts anticipate strong growth potential, while insider stock sale restrictions expire.

title: Samsung triggers a wave of selling in the chip sector, hitting Micron, SanDisk, and global markets
summary: Samsung's weak results cause declines in semiconductor stocks and drag down indices in Wall Street, Europe, and Asia.

title: ECB demands banks have a security plan against AI-driven cyberattacks
summary: Top financial supervisors warn about the growing threat of AI cyberattacks and ask banks for a defense strategy.

title: The drop in crude oil prices opens investment opportunities and stimulates purchases by China
summary: The decline in oil generates investment strategies in the energy sector and leads China to increase its purchases from the Middle East.

title: Rivian announces a public offering of 75 million shares and its stock plummets
summary: The electric vehicle manufacturer places a large number of shares on the market, causing a sharp drop in its stock price.

title: Trump rings Wall Street bell from the Oval Office and Dow Jones hits a new record
summary: The President of the United States participates in the symbolic closing of the market, which reaches a new all-time high at the close.

title: HSBC withdraws from higher-risk private credit lending
summary: The British bank reduces its exposure to private credit funds, reflecting the caution of the financial sector in a high-rate environment.

title: Bitcoin and ethereum open higher this Tuesday
summary: The major cryptocurrencies have a strong start to the day, with positive sentiment in the digital asset market.

title: Ibex 35 closes slightly higher and recovers 19,700 points
summary: The Spanish benchmark rises 0.33% in a session marked by caution over Samsung, SpaceX, and oil, with Telefónica and Cellnex among the top gainers.

title: Bloom Energy and Eos Energy shares capture market attention
summary: While Eos Energy plummets, analysts compare the outlook for both energy sector companies and highlight Bloom Energy's performance.

title: MFE, Inditex, and Endesa distribute dividends among their shareholders
summary: Several Spanish companies announce dividend payments, highlighting Inditex's €1.75 per share approved by its board.

title: Gold and silver trade steady as Citi joins the group of banks that control the London gold market
summary: Precious metal prices remain near $4,200 per ounce for gold and $62 for silver, as Citi enters the London gold clearing club.

title: EasyJet reaches preliminary acquisition agreement worth £5.5 billion with Castlelake
summary: The British low-cost airline could be bought by investment firm Castlelake in a deal that would transform the sector.

title: Adnoc buys Shell's fuel business in South Africa for $1 billion
summary: The UAE state-owned oil company acquires Shell's assets in South Africa, expanding its presence in Africa.

title: Amazon plans $25 billion bond issuance
summary: The e-commerce giant seeks to raise funds in the corporate debt market in one of the largest offerings of the year.

title: Bank of England plans to ease capital rules for the country's banks
summary: The British monetary authority considers easing capital requirements to boost the competitiveness of the financial sector.

title: Coinbase receives UK approval to offer securities trading
summary: The cryptocurrency exchange receives regulatory authorization to operate as a broker in the British market.

title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen convicted of embezzlement
summary: A French court finds Le Pen guilty of irregular use of European funds, which could prevent her from running in the 2027 presidential election.

title: Explosion in Syria during President Macron's visit raises suspicions of attack
summary: An explosion near where the French president was in Syria is being investigated as a possible terrorist attack attempt.

title: Best Buy and Apple warn of price shock for consumers
summary: Retailers anticipate a significant increase in electronics prices due to new tariffs and production costs.
